request.question: Supplementing C57BL/6 mice with Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG for 4 weeks will reduce intestinal permeability by 30% vs controls, measured by FITC-dextran assay, due to upregulation of claudin-1 and occludin.

hypothesis.intervention: Supplementing C57BL/6 mice with Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG for 4 weeks
hypothesis.readiness_rationale: All core fields are present and specific. Hypothesis is CRO-ready today.
hypothesis.literature_search_hint: Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG C57BL/6 mice intestinal permeability FITC-dextran assay
hypothesis.original_hypothesis: Supplementing C57BL/6 mice with Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG for 4 weeks will reduce intestinal permeability by 30% vs controls, measured by FITC-dextran assay, due to upregulation of claudin-1 and occludin.

references.title: Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG treatment improves intestinal permeability and modulates microbiota dysbiosis in an experimental model of sepsis
references.relevance_note: This study demonstrates the effects of Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG on intestinal permeability, directly matching the proposed hypothesis.

references.title: Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG treatment improves intestinal permeability and modulates inflammatory response and homeostasis of spleen and colon in experimental model of Pseudomonas aeruginosa pneumonia.
references.relevance_note: This paper also evaluates the impact of Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG in a murine model, focusing on intestinal permeability, which aligns closely with the experimental context of the hypothesis.

literature_qc.explanation: The retrieved papers include studies that evaluate the intervention of Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG in mouse models, specifically assessing intestinal permeability, which aligns closely with the proposed hypothesis. Papers [2] and [3] both document the effects of Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG on intestinal permeability in experimental models using mice, confirming the intervention and biological system as being the same or similar. Therefore, an exact match is confirmed.
literature_qc.recommended_action: A paper matching this experiment was found in the scientific literature. Review it before proceeding. If this is intentional replication, state that explicitly. If not, differentiate your approach.

protocol_candidates.protocol_name: Open thoracic surgical implantation of cardiac pacemakers in rodents
protocol_candidates.adaptation_notes: The protocol focuses on cardiac pacemaker implantation in rodents and is unrelated to mouse supplementation with Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG or measuring intestinal permeability through a FITC-dextran assay. Significant changes would be needed to redirect this protocol for gastrointestinal studies, including altering the intervention, outcome measures, and overall experimental design.

limitations: Unrelated to the use of probiotics in mice
Does not address intestinal health or permeability measures
Focuses on cardiac physiology rather than gastrointestinal studies

risks.mitigation: Downgrade to pilot execution until at least three biological replicates confirm threshold performance.

risks.description: Control strategy may miss process-specific artifacts in handling or reagent preparation.
risks.mitigation: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.

plan.objective: To determine the effect of Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG supplementation on intestinal permeability in C57BL/6 mice.
plan.experimental_design: Twenty C57BL/6 mice will be randomly divided into two groups: experimental (Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG supplementation) and control (no supplementation). Mice will receive treatments for four weeks, followed by assessment of intestinal permeability using a FITC-dextran assay. The primary measure will be the change in intestinal permeability from baseline levels compared to controls.
controls: C57BL/6 mice receiving 10^8 CFU/mL Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG daily via oral gavage for 4 weeks.
C57BL/6 mice receiving sterile saline solution (0.9% NaCl) daily via oral gavage for 4 weeks.

step_by_step_protocol.description:
DAY 0 (PREP) | 7 days | Animal Acclimatization
Acclimatize 20 C57BL/6 mice in a temperature-controlled room at 22°C with a 12:12 h light/dark cycle for 1 week prior to intervention.
Expected outcome: Mice become accustomed to the experimental environment.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 30 min | Group Assignment
Randomly assign 20 C57BL/6 mice into two groups of 10: Group A (Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG) and Group B (Control).
Expected outcome: Equal distribution of mice between experimental and control groups.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 15 min | Probiotic Preparation
Reconstitute 10^8 CFU/mL Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG in 1 mL of sterile saline solution (0.9% NaCl) for daily oral administration.
Expected outcome: Probiotic solution is ready for administration.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 15 min | Gavage Equipment Preparation
Prepare 20 feeding needles for oral gavage with a diameter of 20G to ensure consistent delivery of treatment.
Expected outcome: Feeding needles are ready for use.

DAY 1 | 30 min | Treatment Administration
Administer 100 µL of the 10^8 CFU/mL Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG solution orally to Group A mice once daily.
Expected outcome: Group A mice receive their daily dose of probiotic.

DAY 1 | 30 min | Control Treatment Administration
Administer 100 µL of sterile saline solution (0.9% NaCl) orally to Group B mice once daily (as control).
Expected outcome: Group B mice receive their daily control treatment.

DAY 29 | 6 h | Preparation for FITC-Dextran Assay
Fast all mice for 6 h prior to the FITC-dextran assay to ensure accurate absorption measurement.
Expected outcome: Mice are fasted and ready for the assay.

DAY 29 | 15 min | FITC-Dextran Administration
Administer 600 µL of 4 kDa FITC-dextran solution (50 mg/mL in sterile PBS) orally to all mice for assessment of intestinal permeability.
Expected outcome: All mice receive FITC-dextran for permeability measurement.

DAY 29 | 1 h | Blood Sample Collection
Collect 200 µL of blood from the tail vein of each mouse 4 hours post-FITC-dextran administration for analysis.
Expected outcome: Blood samples are collected from all mice.

DAY 29 | 10 min | Plasma Separation
Centrifuge the blood samples at 6,000 × g for 10 min at 4°C to separate plasma from cells.
Expected outcome: Plasma is successfully separated for FITC analysis.

DAY 29 | 30 min | Measurement of FITC-Dextran
Measure the fluorescence intensity of the plasma samples using a fluorescence microplate reader at an excitation wavelength of 485 nm and emission wavelength of 528 nm.
Expected outcome: FITC-dextran levels are quantified in plasma samples.

DAY 30 | 1 h | Claudin-1 and Occludin Analysis
Collect intestinal tissue samples from each mouse for Western blot analysis of claudin-1 and occludin protein expression levels.
Expected outcome: Intestinal tissues are obtained for protein analysis.

DAY 30 | 30 min | Protein Extraction from Tissues
Homogenize the intestinal samples in RIPA buffer (250 µL) and centrifuge at 12,000 × g for 10 min at 4°C to extract proteins.
Expected outcome: Proteins are extracted from intestinal tissue samples.

DAY 30 | 90 min | Western Blotting Protocol
Load 30 µg of total protein from each sample into a 10% SDS-PAGE gel; run at 120 V for 90 min for resolution.
Expected outcome: Proteins are resolved in the gel for subsequent transfer.

DAY 30 | 90 min | Transfer Proteins to Membrane
Transfer the resolved proteins to a PVDF membrane at 100 mA for 90 min using wet transfer method.
Expected outcome: Proteins are successfully transferred to PVDF membrane.

DAY 30 | ~16 h | Blocking and Antibody Incubation
Incubate the membranes in blocking solution (5% BSA) for 1 h, then expose to primary antibodies against claudin-1 and occludin (1:1,000 dilution) overnight at 4°C.
Expected outcome: Membranes are blocked and incubating with primary antibodies.

DAY 31 | 2 h | Secondary Antibody Incubation and Detection
Wash membranes and incubate with HRP-conjugated secondary antibody (1:5,000 dilution) for 1 h, followed by detection using ECL substrate for imaging.
Expected outcome: Western blot images capture claudin-1 and occludin levels.

DAY 31 | 2 h | Data Analysis
Quantify fluorescence intensity of FITC-dextran from plasma samples and densitometry of Western blot bands using ImageJ software for statistical analysis.
Expected outcome: Statistical analysis shows intestinal permeability levels and claudin-1/occludin expression.
Mitigation step for RISK-002: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.

assumptions: Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG survives during the gavage and colonizes the gut effectively.
FITC-dextran accurately reflects the level of intestinal permeability.
Claudin-1 and occludin proteins are reliably expressed in response to treatment.
decision_criteria: Proceed if intestinal permeability is decreased by ≥30% in the experimental group compared to controls.
Claudin-1 and occludin levels show a statistically significant increase in the experimental group against controls.
Confirm results using t-tests or ANOVA to establish statistical significance.
Pilot-only mode: do not scale to full run until replicate criteria are met.
risk_mitigations_applied: RISK-001: downgraded to pilot (Downgrade to pilot execution until at least three biological replicates confirm threshold performance.)
RISK-002: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.
reproducibility_notes: Animal housing conditions to be standardized across all batches.
Probiotic source and lot number consistently used for all batches.
FID-dextran assay conditions (stock concentration, incubation times) to remain constant.

validation.primary_endpoint: Post-thaw viability percentage at immediate and 24h checkpoints.
validation.success_threshold: 30% reduction vs controls improvement over comparator in replicate-confirmed data.
failure_conditions: No meaningful viability gain in replicate-confirmed runs.
Intervention increases delayed viability loss at 24h.
validation.suggested_statistical_comparison: Two-group comparison against comparator using t-test or Mann-Whitney based on normality.
validation.minimum_replicates_or_design_note: Minimum three biological replicates before execution-level claim.

cro_ready_brief.objective: To determine the effect of Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG supplementation on intestinal permeability in C57BL/6 mice.
scope_of_work: Execute pilot-first HeLa cryopreservation comparison between DMSO baseline and trehalose arm.
Deliver replicate-confirmed viability analysis and mitigation traceability report.
cro_ready_brief.sample_count: At least 3 biological replicates per arm
cro_ready_brief.materials_responsibility: CRO to quote and confirm all materials before ordering.
questions_for_cro: Can you support controlled-rate freezing logs in each run?
Can you include replicate-level batch metadata in the final report?
